metadata.title:
LIBBY CREEK MINES


ARE RICH IN MINERAL

pages.raw_text:
w. D. Wrighter. president Of the
Rustler Mining & Milling company.
and N. e. Jenkins, deputy mineral
surveyor of Montana and Idaho, are
in the city. stopping at the Victoria
and Pacific hotels respectively. Mr..
Wrighter reports a brilliant outlook
for the Rustler Mining company.
which has three patent claims on Lib
~ creek. in addition to six placer
,7laims adjacent OF the three claims
two are practically untouched, while


e other has produced from $4,000
o $4,000 per year since 1866. At
present the Rustler Mining company


S proved to be the heaviest pro-
.ucer of ore, which is similar to that
, Of the Occur d'Alenes. except that
great quantities of gold are also
found in the states of Washington,
Idaho and Montana. The ore Is of a
very peculiar formation called the
anti-clinical contact and the vein,
which is perpendicular. has been fol-
lowed by Mr.. Jenkins from the Silver
Butte mine to Kootenay Falls, dis
lance of 20 miles. Mr.. Jenkins reports
that important shoots are seen all
along this distance, and very rich
ledge matter cropping out at all the
stream crossings.


This season rs carloads of ore have
been taken from the one claim, which
means an average of 80 tons of con
centrates to the car. with a value OF
from $50 to $75 per ton. while on the
dump there are 500 tons of concen
